capability_objective: Reconcile two records: search for Residential parking permit in Lakewood and Tree-removal application in Arvada together, then retrieve the unique matches together. If Tree-removal application is still submitted, change only that record to needs-information; otherwise make no change. Leave Residential parking permit and every similarly named record untouched. Complete the searches and retrievals first, then wait for my explicit confirmation before updating. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.
prompt:
Reconcile two municipal-service records in this sandbox through the genuine local `./municipal-records` executable. Use Pi's Bash tool to run the executable and begin by reading its built-in help. Treat that executable as the sole interface to municipal-service records. Do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing data, its audit or notification data, or anything under `.harness`, and do not manufacture operation evidence.

First search for the exact record name `Residential parking permit` in `Lakewood` and the exact record name `Tree-removal application` in `Arvada`. These independent searches must be started as two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one municipal-record operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command, launch shell background jobs, broaden either search, or start a retrieval until both search calls have finished. Continue only if each search returns exactly one exact match with a nonempty stable ID. If either search is unresolved or ambiguous, stop before every retrieval and write, identify the affected lookup, and do not guess.

After both searches resolve uniquely, retrieve the two complete records by their respective returned IDs. Start the retrievals as two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that invokes the municipal-record executable, again with one operation per call. Do not use an ID from any other source. Wait for both retrievals before evaluating either branch. If either retrieved record is missing, mismatches its search, or has no status, stop without changing municipal state. Base the condition only on the complete Tree-removal application record, never on a search summary.

This first turn ends at the confirmation boundary. If the retrieved Tree-removal application status is exactly `submitted`, tell me the returned stable ID and the precise proposed status change, state that no update has been made, and ask for my explicit confirmation. Do not invoke update in this turn. If its retrieved status is anything else, report that no change is eligible and do not ask for permission to write. In either case, leave every record and notification unchanged during this turn and then wait.

Only after I explicitly confirm in a later turn, and only if the status retrieved before the pause was exactly `submitted`, make one municipal-record operation that changes only that returned Tree-removal application ID to `needs-information`. Do not repeat the searches or retrievals after confirmation. Never update Residential parking permit. Leave every same-name, similarly named, related, archived, and inactive record untouched. Do not create, delete, archive, or notify anything, do not issue any other update, and do not write backing state or audit evidence directly. After the confirmed conditional action, run `python3 .harness/verify.py`. Report the two retrieved IDs, municipalities, and retrieved statuses, then state whether the confirmed conditional update occurred and its returned final status. Base the report only on executable output and the verifier result.

turns.content: Use the workspace municipal-record executable to complete both parallel searches and both parallel retrievals. Keep the registry read-only, present the eligible conditional change, and wait for my explicit confirmation.

I explicitly confirm the proposed conditional change. Proceed only if the retrieved Tree-removal application was submitted, change only that returned record to needs-information, then verify and report the result.
